public_functions.behavior_description: Initialize a 3-channel complementary, center-aligned PWM using the unified IfxGtm_Pwm driver on the TOM backend. The function declares all configuration structures locally, including the main IfxGtm_Pwm_Config, an InterruptConfig for a period event callback, an array of three channel configuration entries (one per phase pair), and the output pin descriptors for each complementary pair. It initializes the main config with defaults via initConfig, then fills fields to: use the TOM backend in the desired cluster, set frequency to 20 kHz, select center-aligned mode with complementary outputs, enable synchronized start and synchronized shadow updates, and set rising/falling dead-times to 1 µs for each pair. For the three channels (logical indices 0..2), it assigns the high-side output pin and the complementary low-side output pin; sets the initial duty percent values to 25, 50, and 75; and assigns the InterruptConfig pointer to the first/base channel only while the remaining channels have NULL_PTR for their interrupt pointer. The InterruptConfig is fully populated: period mode selected, provider set to CPU0, priority set to 20, periodEvent assigned to IfxGtm_periodEventFunction(void *data) and dutyEvent set to NULL_PTR. Before touching hardware, it prepares all config fields. Then it applies an enable guard: it checks whether the GTM is enabled; if not, it enables the GTM module, reads the module frequency dynamically, and enables the FXCLK clocks via the CMU — all inside the guard block. With the clocks available, it calls the high-level IfxGtm_Pwm_init once, passing a persistent channels array from module state (not a local temporary) so the handle can reference it beyond init. The init applies the initial percent duties atomically to the three complementary pairs. After init, it copies the initial duty percents and any dead-time values into a persistent module-state struct that also holds the PWM driver handle and the channels array for future updates. Finally, it configures the debug/LED GPIO pin as an output (no explicit set level) so the ISR can toggle it on each interrupt event.

Update the three phase duties in percent using a fixed +10% step with wrap-to-0-then-add-step behavior and apply the change immediately via the PWM driver. Specifically: for each of the 3 logical channels (pairs), read the current duty from module state, add the step size (10). If the result is greater than or equal to 100, wrap to 0, then add the same step so the next value becomes the step size. Store the updated percent values back to the module state dutyCycles[3] array. Pass this dutyCycles array directly (percent values, 0..100) to the high-level immediate update API so all three complementary pairs update atomically using synchronized shadow updates.
